name: sr_dsr_access_and_erasure
notes: VT-611 Package Sc #5 — zero-coverage DPDP rail: an ACCESS-style ask (no erasure verb, config/dsr_keywords.yaml's delete_verbs are absent) falls through to the normal brain — expect an honest, capability-bounded answer, never a fabricated literal data dump. An ERASURE ask ('mera data/account delete karo') matches dsr_keywords (a data_nouns token + a delete_verbs token, both required per the VT-585 intent-not-substring fix) and routes DETERMINISTICALLY to dsr_handler (zero-LLM): it creates a dsr_tickets row, freezes autonomy, and sends the team_dsr_acknowledgment template — a RECEIPT, not a completion claim (the actual purge is an async DSR process, not synchronous). The exact template copy isn't verifiable from this repo (Twilio-hosted approved body; SID only in config/twilio_templates.yaml) — treat the acknowledgment's register/tone as a manual transcript-review call; the load-bearing hard assert is the NEGATIVE one: never a same-turn 'erased/deleted successfully' claim. Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 4` then `script <tenant> sr_dsr_access_and_erasure.json`.

steps.message: aap log mere baare mein aur mere customers ke baare mein kya kya data store karke rakhe ho, mujhe pura batao
assert_not_contains: i checked your database and here is everything
steps.note: An ACCESS-style ask, no erasure verb — falls through to the normal brain (config/dsr_keywords.yaml requires a delete_verbs token, absent here). Expect an honest, capability-bounded answer about what categories of data are kept — never a fabricated literal 'here is your full record' dump. Register/completeness is a manual transcript-review call.

steps.message: mera sara data delete kar do aur mera account band kar do
steps.note: An explicit erasure + account-closure ask (data_nouns 'data'/'account' + delete_verbs 'delete'/'band' both present) routes DETERMINISTICALLY to dsr_handler -- a dsr_tickets row + autonomy freeze + the team_dsr_acknowledgment template RECEIPT. Load-bearing negative assert: never a same-turn completion claim -- the purge is an async DPDP process (up to 30 days), never instant, never claimed as done in-chat.
